children.text: each person who acquires, either directly or indirectly, unprocessed timber originating from Federal lands west of the 100th meridian in the contiguous 48 States shall report the receipt and disposition of such timber to the Secretary concerned, in such form as such Secretary may by rule prescribe; except that nothing in this paragraph shall be construed to hold any person responsible for the reporting of the disposition of any such timber held by subsequent persons;

children.label: A
children.text: provide to such other person a written notice, in such form as the Secretary concerned may prescribe, which shall identify the Federal origin of such timber;

children.label: B
children.text: receive from such other person a written acknowledgment of such notice and a written agreement that such other person will comply with the requirements of sections 620 to 620j of this title, in such form as the Secretary concerned may prescribe; and

children.label: C
children.text: provide to the Secretary concerned copies of all notices, acknowledgments, and agreements referred to in subparagraphs (A) and (B);

each person who acquires, either directly or indirectly, unprocessed timber originating from public lands in a State that is subject to an order issued by the Secretary of Commerce under , other than a State that is administering and enforcing a program under , shall report the receipt and disposition of the timber to the Secretary of Commerce, in such form as the Secretary may by rule prescribe, except that nothing in this paragraph shall be construed to hold any person responsible for reporting the disposition of any timber held by subsequent persons; andsection 620c(a) of this titlesection 620c(d) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: provide to such other person a written notice, in such form as the Secretary of Commerce may prescribe, that shall identify the public lands from which the timber originated; and

children.label: i
children.text: a written acknowledgment of the notice, and

children.label: ii
children.text: a written agreement that the recipient of the timber will comply with the requirements of sections 620 to 620j of this title,

children.label: C
children.text: provide to the Secretary of Commerce copies of all notices, acknowledgments, and agreements referred to in subparagraphs (A) and (B).
analyze the effects of indirect substitution on market efficiency;
analyze the effects of indirect substitution on domestic log supply;
offer any recommendations that the Secretaries consider necessary for specific statutory or regulatory changes regarding indirect substitution;
provide summaries of the data collected;
analyze the effects of the provisions of ; andsection 620b(b)(2)(C) of this title
provide such other information as the Secretaries consider appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: If the Secretary concerned finds, on the record and after an opportunity for a hearing, that a person, with willful disregard for the prohibition contained in sections 620 to 620j of this title against exporting Federal timber, exported or caused to be exported unprocessed timber originating from Federal lands in violation of sections 620 to 620j of this title, such Secretary may assess against such person a civil penalty of not more than $500,000 for each violation, or 3 times the gross value of the unprocessed timber involved in the violation, whichever amount is greater.

children.label: i
children.text: Subject to clause (ii), if the Secretary of Commerce finds, on the record and after an opportunity for a hearing, that a person, with willful disregard for the restrictions contained in an order of the Secretary under on exports of unprocessed timber from public lands, exported or caused to be exported unprocessed timber originating from public lands in violation of such order, the Secretary may assess against such person a civil penalty of not more than $500,000 for each violation, or 3 times the gross value of the unprocessed timber involved in the violation, whichever amount is greater.section 620c(a) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: Clause (i) shall not apply with respect to exports of unprocessed timber originating from public lands in a State that is administering and enforcing a program under .section 620c(d) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: assess against such person a civil penalty of not more than $75,000 for each violation if the Secretary determines that the person committed such violation in disregard of such provision or regulation;

children.label: ii
children.text: assess against such person a civil penalty of not more than $50,000 for each violation if the Secretary determines that the person should have known that the action constituted a violation; or

children.label: iii
children.text: assess against such person a civil penalty of not more than $500,000 if the Secretary determines that the person committed such violation willfully.

children.label: i
children.text: Subject to clause (ii), if the Secretary of Commerce finds, on the record and after an opportunity for a hearing, that a person has violated, on or after , any provision of sections 620 to 620j of this title or any regulation issued under sections 620 to 620j of this title relating to the export of unprocessed timber originating from public lands (whether or not the violation caused the export of unprocessed timber from public lands in violation of sections 620 to 620j of this title), the Secretary may assess against such person a civil penalty to the same extent as the Secretary concerned may impose a penalty under clause (i), (ii), or (iii) of subparagraph (A).June 1, 1993

children.label: ii
children.text: Clause (i) shall not apply with respect to unprocessed timber originating from public lands in a State that is administering and enforcing a program under .section 620c(d) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: in determining the applicability of any penalty imposed under this paragraph, shall take into account all relevant mitigating factors, including mistake, inadvertence, and error; and

children.label: aa
children.text: reduce the penalty;

children.label: bb
children.text: not impose the penalty; or

children.label: cc
children.text: on condition of there being no further violation under this paragraph for a prescribed period, suspend imposition of the penalty.

children.heading: .—Contracturalremedies1So in original. Probably should be “Contractual”.1
children.label: ii .—Contracturalremedies1So in original. Probably should be “Contractual”.1
children.text: In the case of a minor violation of sections 620 to 620j of this title (including a regulation), the Secretary concerned shall, to the maximum extent practicable, permit a contracting officer to redress the violation in accordance with the applicable timber sale contract rather than assess a penalty under this paragraph.

children.heading: Penalties not exclusive; judicial review
children.label: 3 Penalties not exclusive; judicial review
children.text: A penalty assessed under this subsection shall not be exclusive of any other penalty provided by law and shall be subject to review in an appropriate United States district court.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), the head of the appropriate Federal department or agency under sections 620 to 620j of this title may debar any person who violates sections 620 to 620j of this title, or any regulation or contract issued under sections 620 to 620j of this title, from entering into any contract for the purchase of unprocessed timber from Federal lands for a period of not more than 5 years. Such person shall also be precluded from taking delivery of Federal timber purchased by another party for the period of debarment.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: No person may be debarred from bidding for or entering into a contract for the purchase of unprocessed timber from Federal lands under subparagraph (A) unless the head of the appropriate Federal department or agency first finds, on the record and after an opportunity for a hearing, that debarment is warranted.

children.heading: Withholding of awards during debarment proceedings
children.label: ii Withholding of awards during debarment proceedings
children.text: The head of an appropriate Federal department or agency may withhold an award under sections 620 to 620j of this title of a contract for the purchase of unprocessed timber from Federal lands during a debarment proceeding.

children.heading: Cancellation of contracts
children.label: 2 Cancellation of contracts
children.text: The head of the appropriate Federal department or agency under sections 620 to 620j of this title may cancel any contract entered into with a person found to have violated sections 620 to 620j of this title or regulations issued under sections 620 to 620j of this title.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: e Exception
children.text: Subsections (c) and (d) do not apply to violations of .section 620i of this title
